reviews.review_text: We found the cabin to be comfortable if run down. Sadly the floor was covered in grit, presumably sand. And the bed had a liberal sprinkling between the sheets.  Not clear if hadn’t been fully cleaned, but was unsettling. Pool is delightful, shaded by palms and tall trees. Had to drive past their rubbish pile to get to cabin and road potholed and pitted. So. Run down, clearly, but decent price and other than cleanliness was okay.
Camping bien situé et calme

Good spot, we got a premium cabin. Heaps of room aircon on and was nice a cool too walk in too. Shower wasn’t great head was slightly damaged and water sprayed out into the main bathroom area otherwise not bad. Beds are okay for a holiday park comfy enough for a good nights sleep but not the most comfortable, provided nice warm blanket and sheets where clean tidy. Floors were abit dirty after shower my feet became dirty again from walking around inside. Overall good experience
Great for kids - pools, jumping pillow and water play park, clean and spacious cabins with most things we need (missing sharp knife, chopping board, grater). Huge park.

We had a wonderful time at Howard Springs, the managers Stacy and Mark are lovely people. Our dog appreciated the fact that there wasn’t a single bindy, prickle or nasty in the grass it was lovely and lush. We highly recommend the park for anyone who is visiting Darwin.

absolutely horrible attitude and customer service. I have arrived 20.minutes past 5 with campervan. They turned me away that I arrived after hours, not offering any solution, basically hanged up the phone. Nicely done.

Howard Springs is a good location for me as the area is quiet and close to where my daughter lives. The staff are slways friendly and helpful. The cabins are a kways clean. It would be a great family friendly park for the children.

2 bedroom was filthy with mould and grime everywhere from the bed sheets to the windows and cockroachs to boot.
Had to wash and rewash the cutlery and pans befor using.
Pool was big and enjoyable with lots of kids but with little shade made the pool warm. Location is somewhat out of town and main points of interest.

It's 6pm and I'm sitting here in the shade enjoying the beautiful wet season's warmth and the serenity of this parks environment, listening to the bird life singing.  We've This been here just on two weeks now, and this facility is awesome, while there's still a little bit of work needed around the site, that doesn't interfere with anything.  The services here are really good, water, power, amenities, pools and parks for the kids.  I gave 4 stars for the location only because of the amount of traffic passing by first thing in the morning otherwise it would definitely have been a five.  Absolutely love this place!! 😀 thanks fellas

Was very disappointed in the actions of a staff member on a Sunday afternoon shift. Was abusive and disrespectful towards park guests over a minor issue. Made guests feel uncomfortable. Could have quite easily handled issues in a different manor but chose to belittle others.

DONT STAY HERE
I booked a room and after arriving late, despite notifying that plane delayed, NO KEYS, NO SERVICE.
Only phone number was for maintenance.
Late Night and no room for myself and Kids... Managers didn't care. Plane delayed in Melbourne for over an hour due to a passenger issue…

Had come for family tragedy with 2 little girls and a friend… I have never been so stressed….
Phoned Berry Springs and booked into a cabin despite the late hour, friendly and helpful staff. Book at Berry Springs AOK.
Nice and quiet....no frills. Kids will love the little water park.
Lovely park  We extended our stay for 2 weeks Enjoyed the larger pool ( there's 2) Children friendly park.  Very friendly and helpful staff.  Thankyou for our enjoyable stay

Great staff and manager. Clean and maintained.
Safe too.

We stayed here for a least two weeks on a caravan site. There’s lots of trees, we prefer sites with no trees. If you ask they are more than accommodating to help you get a site that suites your needs. I enjoyed the adults pool, for a bit of peace and quiet.
We looked after our van and car while we flew out for seven nights in Bali. Lovely friendly staff that work hard to maintain the staff.

We arrived late, check in was OK but when we arrived at the cabins which were not cheap we were highly disappointing😞. The couch was filthy, the walls had dirt & bits of something on them. The handrail outside the cabin was so rotten that was unhinged loose., kitchen chairs dirty, lack of utensils. Not enough crockery for 6 in a 6 berth cabin. Remotes didn't work airconds and not enough blankets. When we contacted park ppl they did supply extra linen. 1st group was moved as there cabin was disgusting still had dirt on sheets, shrubbery inside the beds arghh...2nd group got a reclean again dirty linen/beds, 3rd group cleaned there own cabin but required clean linen. When we brought further attention to the conditions we were met with "if u don't like it then leave" overall the park could be a jewel of a place with effort to fix things up example pool tiles, laundry area. Management (woman) rude and no empathy. I do not recommend this park.

We stayed for 3 weeks, absolutely wonderful stay. Shady sites, lovely grassed areas, really great pools. The bird life around is wonderful to listen to. Such a relaxing 3 weeks. Will definitely come here when we return to the NT.

Called to book a cabin and was advised i wasn't allowed to have a 5th person (infant in portacot we supply) as they are for 4 people max. We were looking to stay for a week so have had to book elsewhere. Poor form.

I stayed here a couple of times, plenty of room in cabins and sites close  to local hotel and 30-45 minutes from Darwin City close to wetlands to sightseeing or goose hunting. Gateway plaza is close to…
Awesome park, warm pools,  adults only pool, clean amenities, friendly staff. Loved the park

We booked a 2 bedroom cabin  with a queen bed in one bedroom and a double and single bunk  upon arrival I noticed only the queen bed was made up with bed sheets and not the other bedroom, I rang them to tell them and their response was there's only 2 of you,  and I said but I booked a 2 bedroom and would like the beds to be made up ,not very happy to oblige but made up bed which took a lot of effort,  looked at me and thought about it and her response was "are you sleeping separately and my reply YES ! I should not have to explain my sleeping arrangements with them but was told that I should have notified them which I think it's ridiculous , so infact had I paid for a 2 bedroom and was expected to sleep in one bedroom. In that case I would have booked only  1 bedroom cabin , not sleep in one bedroom and pay for 2 bedroom, and there was the bathroom towel issue only 2 bath towels no hand towels of face washers. asked if I could have hand towels only given one I said can I have another one as there is  2 of us looked at me as this was a big effort on her behalf to get me another hand towel. I mention to her that we have stayed there before about 4 years ago and did not have this problem with the towels or the bedroom lined. was told that it has always been that way  I think not ! We have stayed at other Big 4 s and in a 2 bedroom cabin towes supplied on all beds and bed linen in all regardless weather slept on of not. Bottom line is we booked and paid for  a 2 bedroom. The cabins need a lot of work looking very run down holes in walls and uncomfortable , could have at least a 2 seater couch in them sitting on plastic chairs is not great. I would not recommend this caravan park for cabin use, if you book for a 2 bedroom make sure the beds are all made up and bath towels supplied. A very disappointed stay this time.

I am writing to share the profoundly negative experience we had upon our arrival. We reached the reception desk at 5:03 PM precisely, only to be met with immediate hostility from the woman in charge.
​Before we could even explain our situation, she shouted at us that the reception was closed. We calmly tried to explain that we were in an emergency situation, hoping for a shred of understanding or assistance. Instead, we were met with a complete refusal to even consider a solution. There was no discussion, no flexibility, and certainly no compassion.
​Working in the tourism and hospitality industry requires a fundamental appreciation for human contact and a capacity for empathy. The individual we encountered possessed neither. Her behaviour was not just unprofessional; it was deeply unkind. One has to wonder about the kind of life that leads to such a cold and dismissive attitude towards people in need.
​I genuinely suggest she reconsiders her career path, as she is clearly unsuited for any role that requires customer service. This was a truly shocking and disheartening welcome.

I arrived at this caravan park at 5:04 pm, just a few minutes after reception had closed. The lady at the desk opened the door only to tell me they were closed, even though I simply asked for an unpowered site for one night. She said she couldn’t do anything. It’s very frustrating to see such a lack of effort — five minutes shouldn’t make the difference between helping a customer or turning them away. Very poor service.

Nice spot, but find it rather odd that they tell you on the paper they give you with information that the laundry is only $6 but charge $7.01 that's just outright robbery and false advertising they should look into changing that immedietly.

Tjete are many layers to this park, great for families and children. Dog friendly but ostracised to one area of park with signs reminding you of same. Most washing machines had been out over order for months and $6 per load if and when you could get one. Boom gate constantly malfunctions.  If you need to use an uber for grocery run they wont allow the driver to bring you or your groceries through the boom gate!  Some trees have huge hard cricket like fruit that drop from a great height. one guy had his dolar panel cracked. Expensive for what you get.

We stayed on a caravan site grassed area for 27days. Very happy with everything.
Staff are so friendly & helpful. They keep the park clean & sites mowed. Close to all shopping and 20min drive to Darwin cbd.
Excellent caravan park 4 pools massive BBQ area really enjoyed staying here and would definately come back

Loved the caravan park, the staff were helpful and friendly. We had a great site, the peacocks were very friendly, as was the little possum who visited us a couple of nights. We felt safe and secure there. They have a coffee van on site and offered Cambodian street food on the Saturday night.
We used our own facilities so I can't comment on theirs.
The only negative comment I would make is about the boom gates - they are very slow, the entry pin pad is very loose and wobbles about, and the exit boom gate took absolutely ages to lower when the car in front had gone through. So we spent more time than necessary at the boom gates.
Other than that - we loved our stay there - it wasn't far from all the places we had planned to visit during our visit to Darwin.

The park itself is very run down. We were there for over 2 weeks and saw overgrown plants all over the cabins and air conditioners and through out the park, fallen palm fronds etc|Reception staff presented as defensive and miserable|Amenities are very run down and not very clean|Bare minimal washing machines which meant queues|We hired a cabin for 6 nights while our daughter visited. The sheets and pillow cases had visible dirt on them and a large yellow stain on the top sheet. We took everything off and used our own sheets and bedding.|Small sites, rules not enforced - many dogs off lead, barking and being left unattended.
Great venue, spacious toilets and showers. Clean with lot of room for campsites.

We came to Howard Springs caravan park to meet up with some friends who were caravanning around Australia.|We stayed in a ensuite cabin with a seperate bedroom and bunk beds situated in the hall way.|The cabin had all that you need for a comfortable stay.|The park is set in a nice setting and is not overly large so you can get around easily.|There are two pools to pick from and one is adults only,there also plenty of facilities for young ones playground,games room,water playground.|The park is not to far from town and also nearby are  the highways access to NT  national parks etc

I arrived on the 5th of July and left on the 19th of July|Whilst there an electrician was working on the power supply that we were connected to, turning the power off and on numerous times, we thought nothing of this until it was time to leave|Went to pull the power cable out of the caravan and one of the pins from the caravan had fused to the power lead|contacted them via the phone, no answer|sent them a reply email to the one they send you asking how your stay was|No reply|contacted head office,they said that they are individually franchised|So there as a power surge, and it is Gunna cost me a considerable amount|Left a very bad taste in my mouth and will not recommend this park to anyone e speak to

Would not recommend. Severely lacking maintenance. So jammed in had to ask other guests to move their cars just so we could hitch
Boom gate constantly fails, was a bit concerned we we wouldn’t be able to early leave for next destination
Only two washing machines working all others out of order
Dirty water - constantly blocking our filter and constantly had to turn and rinse out
Power outages
Broken water pipe leaking water on the road near the amenities
Damage to vans due tree dropping huge nuts. One van had smashed solar panel
Had an ensuite site, tight to say the least.  Some parts of the park are open and spacious, but the section we were in was tired and in need of updating.

Great Park to stay, has everything you need and want and more. We originally booked for 8 nights and ended up staying 15 nights.
Mark was a fabulous host and was willing help when needed.
Coffee van in the park was a winner, friendly folk and great coffee and yummy cookies.
Ensuite style amenities are a winner for me! Pools are fabulous, camp kitchen well set up.
Our site had lovely green grass, was well shaded and close to everything in the park.
Pet Friendly and had a Dog Sitter 2 streets away at a great price.
It was an easy straight run into Darwin from here also.
I would definitely recommend this park!! Steve & Rhonda

We made a book with Howard Springs and drove from Katherine to Darwin only to receive a text 2 hours before check-in that they had cancelled our booking.
An accidental email was sent to us stating that this was a known issue with the third party booking system that they use, however, we have still driven 330km to get here.
Please ensure that if you book online that you call them to confirm the booking before leaving your destination.
We camped as we are travelling,  there are heaps of different accommodation  styles available though , beautiful park , great for couples and families alike .

Shower blocks were unclean, no water pressure, dodgy locks in cubicles (our 8y.o got stuck in a shower cubicle!)
Washing machine facilities were by far the worst we’ve seen, 75% were out of order. When reported to staff, staff reciprocated with a counter complaint about their own head office & how that they had to wear all the complaints.
Boom gate system is so antiquated, it caused delays & lock outs on every entry/exit. When assistance requested from staff, staff blamed user error. Generally staff were not helpful and did not demonstrate good customer service.
Overall, this caravan park was grotty, grubby & lacked attention to detail. We would not recommend this accommodation.

The park needs YLC, washing machines  out of order, amenities  block very basic, som shower heads  u/s, the boom gates are a pain in the  arse, big dangerous  pot holes, that doesn't  take much to fix, numerous  powered sites no grass, a dust bowl, Big 4 used tobe one off the top caravan parks,  no longer.
We had an amazing stay here we stayed on a powered site and was a beautiful spot so much fun splash park pools ride on karts pet friendly spots it was that great we extended our stay thank you 😊

Our second visit to Darwin but first in this park.   We love Darwin.  And we thoroughly enjoyed our stay at this Big4.  The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful, we had a great roomy site.  Whilst we did not swim, the pools both looked very inviting.   Enjoyed the company if the resident peacocks with my morning yoga.
A great safe location when visiting Darwin. Far enough out of the city to feel secure but an easy drive in and out.
So many activities for kids highly recommend. Sites are quite small, but manageable
Small caravan sites. People walking across our sites. Loved the ensuite bathrooms.

Friendly and helpful staff at reception and maintenance crew! While this is a good park location wise to lots of shops, Howard Tavern and short drive to Darwin city it could do with more maintenance in terms of fixing broken washing machines and throughly cleaning the ensuites, also fill the many large pot holes!
I wonder why they thought it was useful to put a broken sign on one of the washing machines advising that it was broken by guests, why not just fix it so new guests can use it!
This park is beautiful. Such beautifully maintained sites. Friendly staff and peaceful. There is a quiet adult pool and lots to do if you want to join in. Would rate it best in Darwin.
Good sized Van site easily accessible. Staff very approachable and friendly. Park is set up well with heaps for kids to keep occupied. Will stay again when next in Darwin.

Loved our powered site here close to many shops easy to navigate your site.
Plenty of assistance backing in lots of green grass Friendly staff Coffee van onsite and the best bacon and egg muffins from another onsite vendor.
Walking distance to the tavern or courtesy bus.

You should Book ahead rather than be disappointed

Great park. Nice pools. Washing machines are dodgy though, stole my money. Great hosts and friendly staff. Loved the individual shower and loo amenities. Fab little IGA 1km walk or bike and the tavern is there too.
Great park, big sites, family atmosphere, friendly staff
Great place for a Darwin stay
Great place to stay. We booked for 6 nights and stayed for 12. Lovely grassy sites. Clean amenities. Great access to shopping centres and easy to get into Darwin. Will definitely stay there again.
We stayed for 3weeks.Fantastic service and fantastic amenities.Best place to stay

This place is the worst place I have ever stayed at. The cabin we are staying in is absolutely terrible the bed is so tiny me and wife don’t fit smallest double I ever seen. I’m 6’1 and my feet hang over the edge of the bed as well. The fridge is tiny can’t fit much food in there at all. There is no tongs or much cooking appliances and utensils. The range hood don’t work either the fan only works on level 3 as well. The beds ain’t that comfy either very hard and ridged also we had an ant infestation as well. We moved the couch and found an old dry spill that they never bothered to clean we had to clean it. Don’t leave food unwrapped ants will get into it as well. They never made the top bunk bed. Would not recommend these cabins at all was a complete disaster. Pools are absolutely freezing and dirty as well. Splash pool was hardly ever on. Water got turned off twice in the 14 days we stayed here as well.

Not good. Booked online for 4 nights, but were refused entry as we had not booked a dog friendly site. Online site did not make that clear.Not very helpful  and no refund. Went up the road to Coolalinga Tourist Park,which was friendly and welcoming.

The grounds are well maintained, however the staff are rude & reluctant to help in any form. They allocate you a spot then squash you in like sardines, you are that close to your fellow travellers that you can hear them fart, I would highly suggest don’t use this park.

Safe
Great place
Good clean facilities
Good location
Very friendly staff
Found the office staff very professional and a delight to deal with. They did everything to meet my requirements

Rooms so small old fridge falling apart. Double bed ridiculous in this day and age for a couple.  Limited storage for extended stay with a suite case each. The park leaves a lot to be desired.  Kids running ferrel no parental supervision. Wouldn't recommend
Out of date most run down big 4 i have ever been in,i hope its not the new big 4 standard.
Great place to stay 20 kilometres but the drive was fine the staff were very helpful and friendly would recommend to everyone thank you

Someone suggested to stay temporarily at Big4 while we were hunting for a rental home . We stayed over a month and we had an amazing experience. Kids friendly, Stacy was excellent. Except for a handful of hiccups we had a great stay.

Very nice park with lovely swimming pool and kitchen facilities. The cabin was clean and well equipped. My only fault was the smoke alarm which went off a few times during the night and there was no one to call to come and fix it. Difficult to walk around the park as is so wet from all the sprinklers
Had a site with slab, beautiful park, pool great . Sites a bit tight. Amenities clean and tidy . Easy drive to town and pub just up the road with great service .

After waiting 45mins on the highway, we were told that the ensuite site wasn’t ready. I had explained that I didn’t care about the ensuite, I just wanted to unhook the van as I needed to be somewhere and had no more time to spare. The 2 women at reception then refused us to stay because I was annoyed. Even though I got a refund, it was not the point. After staying at several parks throughout our trip, this is by far the worst park. I would never consider this place ever again. Reception staff are slow and clearly don’t care about their customers.

Wow for an accidental booking we've had the most amazing stay here in the "honeymoon suite" the staff are all fabulous, the amenities are spotless and the location is amazing. We don't want to leave! The staff made our anniversary fabulous, thanks so much guys, I have no doubt we ll be back ☺️

Mark(?) the older man showing people where to park was so rude to my neighbour that my neighbour up and left after one night. He paid for seven. The sites were minuscule, cramped in like sardines yet the other part of the park was empty. Too lazy to spread us all out. More work for them. Only found one working washing machine. Pool was nice but the shower there had long given up the ghost. Aged and tired park with a staff member that needs the sack.
I’d give it minus stars if I could.

Visited from SA and we camped with our tent booked an ensuite site! Easy check in, great pools splash pad and playground and easy to use washing machines!  We loved our stay here and would stay again! Friday night they had a food van onsite with delicious Colombian food!
Ensuite bathroom was clean and tidy and loved our location close to amenities!

Location is out of the city but not far and close to the croc parks! And other tourist locations!

Looking forward to our trip next year again!

Pour des umpowered vraiment ok, douche aussi et gérant drole dans sa golfette
Par contre la wifi est pas top

We were booked in here in our caravan for 3 nights, amazing park, nice clean amenities and the service from the manager was 5 stars. He even helped us set up our Starlink properly. I would recommend this park to anyone travelling to Darwin.
Krásny caravan park s ponukou rôznych zážitkov, hlavne spojených s vodou.... Obsluha na recepcii trochu laxný prístup ale inak super....

After weeks Roof top tenting the thought of a cabin was bliss. Sadly we were disappointed! Yes the bed was clean and comfortable the shower also, mold free, but the rest of the cabin needs total overhaul very worn and smelly. Entry steps extremely dangerous. Laundry had the usual dramas of broken machines. We loved the pool, the camp kitchen looked great. On a drive around the park on our way out, it was very evident we were unlucky we were in the old tied area. Pitt Street needs lots of TLC .

Beautiful site with 2x pools, heaps of room for everyone.  Pet friendly.  Good cooking facilities. Friendly staff. Small water park for the kids.
Believe the park has cleaned up their act from what I see on other reviews. Only negative is pools close stupid early and once office closes at 7:30 it's fend for yourself if you need any help. Sign on office door states "don't call unless it's an emergency". Needed help with a situation and they didn't want to help until the morning if I hadn't "already sorted it yourself "

Big4, Has always prided it self on spectacular family friendly parks, well not this one, for a Big4 it is a disgrace.
Maintenance is non existent, filthy smelly camp kitchen, fridges are black with mould, BBQ has grease and fat running into floor which is attracting vermin.
Grass is knee high everywhere, trees overgrown.
Weeds and foliage growing out of cabin gutters.
Laundry areas have no working machines and vermin running in and out.
The photographs of this park on your Big4 site are absolutely false and do not depict its current run down filthy state.
Big4 Management Group or Board you better get out there and sort it or remove your name from it before irreparable damage is done to your reputation.

We stayed at Howard Springs Big4 in Sept-Oct 2024 & it still haunts us! The reviews here are very real!
On arrival the manager told us how he kicked out bikies, was stabbed by druggies, but won that fight, & was in cahoots with local police. What the???
Four wks later, we were falsely accused by another caravaner (who had already told us he'd tried to book our spot) that our dogs attacked his, & we punched him 🤦‍♀️With sev witnesses to the actual incident, this was quickly resolved by weekend managemnt. However on Mon, park management (him-her couple) took it upon themselves to kick us out!!! We requested police attendance to report the so-called incident so that we all could report facts, but they refused!
Since then they have put false defaming notes on our file which has prevented us from renting any other Big4 site in Australia!!!
Stay away! Stay well away from this caravan park!
Kids loved the pools

Nice location, out of town but close enough to shops. Some shady caravan sites, all very close together. Good pools, one adults only;
Amenities old & run down & not always clean. Washing machine & vending machine ate my money for no return. Park facilities old & broken. Apparently "in the process of renovating", but a long termer told us that line is at least 12mths old! Also, bad luck if you're not a dog fan. Squish dog people in with non dog people! Had 2 yappers one side that just wouldn't shut up.
Most office staff nice, but regular one was 'cold' & couldn't smile. Grounds Manager came & checked on us first morning & didn't stop talking, telling us how tough he was & who'd he'd kicked out! Not sure if he was trying to scare us away, or just discipline us before kids acted up?
Wanted to give it 2.5 stars, but 2 will do. Plenty of other nicer experiences to stay around same area!

First the good stuff 👌
The budget cabins are clean and well fitted out although a few extra items like a cork screw and grater would be handy, but otherwise kitchen has all you could need for a budget cabin they do well!
Great soaps nice, warm showers really clean bathrooms 😊.
Good beds and fantastic a/c.

The staff are fun and friendly and really warm. I havnt had such lovely staff anywhere I've stayed and I am always travelling. Give them all a raise!!! Fantastic!

We live 10 mins away from this park and staycation here when we get a chance.
This time I brought friends from Single Parent Adventures Australia to do a group event.
We were excited for the recreation room, squash, splash park, 2 pools, tons of pedal bikes and hopefully jumping pillow.
Alas-
The rec room is not functional and seems mid demolish.
The squash courts are also undergoing maintenance (they were last year too- at this point maybe just say they don't exist?)
The jumping pillow is gone- I expected as much as last year it was having issues.
Only 2 pedal bikes were operational and one of them seemed to be having issues which is fine unless you brought a heap of familys expecting enough to go around. We shared the 2 between 4 familys and made it work still had a great time.
The splash park isn't working properly and puddle park would be a better description. Again we still managed to have fun but the bucket isn't working and the water in the rest was often barley a trickle. Great for the toddlers not so much the kids who need more stimulation. We did try the button it was not effective.
One pool is now adults only which I actually think is amazing 👏 Even I as a parent like the idea of a safe space to go hide in 🤣
The other pool was spectacular! The spas are amazing to relax in while the kids used the huge pool.
All in all we had a blast but being locals who stayed BECAUSE of all the entertainment for kids. I feel a bit duped.
Maybe update the website to reflect all the broken/not operational things.
Or reflect it in price.
We probably won't be back for a while untill it's all fixed as there are other places with operational facilities. Being that we live 10 mins away and didnt NEED to stay it was a big expense for so many broken attractions.

Absolutely superb park and location - not far from anything.
My dog loves the off lead area for him, it's huge.

Clean amenities
Trees for shade
Some drive thru sites
2 pools
Kids water Park
Lovely park we stayed 16 nights had a nice shady spot for the van a dog park for the dogs 2 pools one was very shady the other had no shade nice clean amenities friendly people we felt very safe

~ pool opening times disappointing. 9am to 6pm. Why are grownups not allowed to swim outside these hours when it’s in the middle to high 30s and extremely humid.
~ cheek-by-jowl camps
~ No seats in some of the shower blocks - too bad for disabled and elderly
~ soap holders in showers were filthy
~ open laundry - very dusty and dirty.

Very big dog run provided you have dogs larger than a Jack Russell. My dog easily found her way out. I did try to cover up the openings with palm fronds but they were promptly taken away.

To say we were disappointed would be the understatement of the century.  The ensuite for our camper trailer was dirty and the space so small we couldn't park our car with our camper.
The noise from the stupid birds squawking all night was enough to drive you mad... we stayed 3 out of the 4 nights booked it was just not worth staying another night.
The only nice thing in the whole place was the swimming pool  which our little one enjoyed.
Not exactly BIG 4 flagship....

Excellent. Loved our stay in Howard Springs. A one week stay turned into 4. Staff were all lovely. Had great banter with manager. Got to know manager after being a passion fingers and his regular visits to make sure I hadn't broken anything else...lol. We will definitely be back.
Great pool and right near Maccas and nearby shops

Wat een troosteloze camping. Erg verouderd, ook onze ensuite. Caravan van de rechterburen stond over de grens heen en de haringen van de linkerburen stond ook over de grens. We hadden amper plek en geen privacy. Oude en vieze wasmachines. Vuilnisbakken vol en nooit leeggemaakt dus iedereen plaatst zijn vuilnis erop of ervoor. Staanplaatsen met vaste caravans met vuilnis, troep en zeilen om de regen tegen te houden. Asfalt door het park vergaan en niet overal aanwezig. 1 van de zwembad vol blaadjes en niet goed onderhouden. Splashpark is leuk voor kleine kinderen maar ook erg verouderd. Wat een ellende plek, had liever 0 sterren gegeven.
We recently had a 5 week stay here and can highly recommend the place. Stacy and her staff were fantastic to deal with, roomy sites and felt very safe
Great caravan.nice pools.Great service

We were on a powered caravan site ideally located near all amenities. Very dog friendly and the coffee from the vehicle is a godsend in the morning. Overall thoroughly recommend though it is quite a way from Darwin’s CBD.
Jammed in like sardines and water in the showers were cold

We stayed 9 nights.  On arrival, I think it might be the management lady was rude.  The man was lovely and acknowledged me, and was helping me but she cut him off  and told him to answer a telephone over serving me.  She didn’t acknowledge me or step in to help.  I had to wait until the young receptionist took over after the call she was on.   The man guiding us to our spot was lovely.

The sites are horrendously small! If someone else was to park where they were meant to on the site next to us they would have literally been on our caravan mat plus there wasn’t a car space allocated to our spot and we were uncomfortably close to the person behind us.

The washing machines are over priced, At $6 a go + most the machines are broken and those that were ‘Working’ didn’t have a spin cycle at the end and the clothes come out dripping wet and you had to hand wring them all out.

Water pressure is terrible, some days/times you are lucky to get a dribble out of the tap.

Certainly has improvement needed.

Stayed here for just over a week in a powered site. First site allocated would not fit out van, however we were promptly allocated another site with no issues. Water is connected to mains so no issues with that despite being worried at first due to older reviews. Pools and spas are great. Splash park is good too despite being a little dated. Small park for the kids. Park itself and amenities were clean. fairly quiet apart from some noise from birds. Whole place is a little dated but nothing wrong with that. No jumping pillow or squash courts as shown in some places on the internet. 20 mins out of Darwin, with a nice easy drive. Close to a few shopping centers and Howard Springs Nature Park. At time of writing $85 per night powered site. Always felt safe here.

Staying here for a week in July 2024 on a powered site. It’s a lovely park with helpful staff and clean, convenient amenities. There are two swimming pools and a splash park for little kids. We highly recommend this park and look forward to staying here again.

Grassed sites although a lot are largely brown and straw like in the dry season. The pool is good, slightly warmer than most we came across in NT caravan parks. Hardly noticed air traffic or road traffic, but there are consistent barking dogs from somewhere near the park. This noise travels. You hear them intermittently all day. The CP owners could consider tracking down where this is coming ftom to get this controlled or engage the Council. The old squash courts could be pulled down and a smaller amenities unit built to free up some space and give the main pool area a better feel. Its good that you are escorted to your site and helped to park as when the park was a bit busier some sites can be tight to get into. Generally you are allocated a site with a good amount of room for your setup.
It was a very nice park with great facilities.  The staff were most helpful and certainly will go there again.
The pools and splash Park are great for the kids. Amenities seemed pretty clean and serviced. Heaps of sires in the park.

We had a great site no complaints. It was peaceful despite the air traffic. My only criticism is the amenities block. It was always dirty. I get it's hard to keep clean 24/7 but we were there for over 3 weeks. The ensuites need a good clean. The dirt build up behind toilets and sinks is not great. Perhaps you could leave more than 1 dry mop out. I would gladly mop out after my use. Also need more washing machines.
Had a great stay here.
We stayed here for 2.5 weeks on a powered site, the park is beautiful, the amenities are clean, the staff are lovely. 10/10 highly recommend.

A piece of " Paradise " awaits, as the friendly / attentive and accommodating staff make your arrival easy and  "Welcome" you with relaxed smiles 😃.
Once through the boom gates, you drive into a park, full of nature, palms,🌴 beautiful flowering trees, and rainforest scrubs 🌺.
Beautiful green grass.
3 pools that are Salt water 💧,  but I will say " the secluded pool near the boom gate is a lot cooler than the big one, 😎 but both have cascading water that flows over rocks  🪨 into the pool as a waterfall .
I watched  children play in the Waterpark, and the excitement and laughter and fun was had by all involved, as the bucket filled and everyone got wet 🤷🤣
The amenities, toilet blocks/showers, washing machines, dryers, vending machines all clean, working, and well maintained.
Clothes lines
All BBQ areas are clean and maintained, and these plenty throughout the massive grounds along with picnic tables to enjoy your meal.
The park has its own very friendly Peacocks 🦚 that have wondered the park, and enjoyed interacting with  visitors 😍
A BIG thank you to " MARY" for that amazing cuddle, it was a tough day, and this amazing lady, made me feel not so alone.

Our trip here, was the start of my 22 year old sons final wish, after being diagnosed with Sarcoma Matasisized cancer.
I don't want to leave.
We are so blessed 🙌.
EXCELLENT destination 👌
Excellent caravan park with large grass sites, clean amenities and very helpful staff.

We stayed at the park for just over a month Really great atmosphere great Park nice to see green Grass. Only a short distance in to Darwin .Great pub just up the road Mark and Chris The ladies in the office Really made it a great time Highly recommended will definitely will be back . Mal and Cyan

This is a very large park with cabins glamping tents powered and non powered sites.  Location is good and quiet at nighttime.  There are loads of wildlife in the park and a flock of peacocks.  The curlews can be a little noisy at night.  The place needs a bit of an overhaul and tidy up.  Amenities  seemed somewhat clean.  The water pressure is horrendous but l understand they are working on this.  The tavern down the road does good meals and has theme nights.  We probably wouldn't stay here again.  Not sure if the other parks are any better.  The tropical environment is harsh on infrastructure.  The current caretakers are doing their best. Close to many shopping centre's and around 20kms from the centre of Darwin.
Lovely staff. Wonderful pool's and water splash park & playground for kids
Excellent cabins for anyone travelling with a dog
Great caravan park, cabins are clean and comfortable. Great for kids with the splash park and pools.
No water in campkitchen working. 50 dollars for unpowered site is too much.

Great location for a quick getaway.
Bed was very comfortable.
Staff very friendly and helpful.
Loved this place. The location, scenery, convenience. Def would stay again.
I stayed a week and found the staff to be very accommodating allowing me to use shelter for my boat.
Good pools, playground and water park...little ones were looking forward to the bouncy pillow, website and facebook photos need updating.

We stopped in for 2 nights & were going to check into another park as we had booked it because it was closer to where we needed to be. Cancelled that booking and extended our stay at Howard springs to 2 weeks. We did not want to leave. 23ft van with so much space around us, green grass, palm trees, great amenities everywhere throughout the park, pools were beautiful with the bonus of spas in amongst the rocks. Kids loved the splash park, service station next door for gas bottles and other essentials, Howard springs tavern down the road with amazing meals in the dining room. Overall an amazing stay, grounds staff and cleaners work super hard to keep everything beautiful. 💯 enjoyable stay.
Very friendly, peaceful family location.

I arrived 39mins after closing  but was kindly  checked in. Lots after big trees for shade. Individual bathrooms are great. Unfortunately the peacocks were very  noisy  and I am a light sleeper. Bushstone Curlews also had a bit to say but were not as intrusive  as the peacocks.

Unfortunately, I was called asking for money when I had already paid the correct amount, the fact that there was no drinking water was a huge inconvenience, the splash park wasn't working for 2 of the days which the kids were disappointed about, the rec room was really dirty, I was sent a message about checking out by 10am a day too early and overall the place is pretty run down. Staying here was a luxury for us and cost us a fortune.  We prefer free cos but decided to splurge because my mum was coming to visit. Disappointing 😞.
All quite good
This is a great stop in the top end. Facilities are excellent, staff are enthusiastic and attentive. Nearby shops, tavern and mechanical services. The resident bird life add a special dimension.

I didn’t really want to write this review. I thought I would give the management time to respond to my complaints, but despite true promises they have done nothing!!!
We stayed in cabin 32 for 2 weeks at the princely sum of $4700 for a 3 bedroom cabin.
At that price you expect things to work and the cabin to at least be cleaned!!
Not so…
Upon arrival we found the curtains hanging off their rails, two of the ceiling lights not working, the oven not working, the washing machine not working, the floor dirty and in dire need of a clean ( which we did!) only one smoke alarm working, and the lounge glass coffee table falling to bits, ready to smash at any time!
We informed the management about so of this and during the course of our two week stay they attempted and failed with some feeble repairs.
At the end of our stay we complained Again about all of this and they said they would get back to us within 24 hours…. It’s now been a week and counting and we have heard nothing!!!

Terrible facility and terrible service!!
Was a great, family friendly accommodation! The kids loved the splash park and pool. Would definitely stay here again when travelling with the family.

Location was perfect for what we needed.
Staff were incredible upon checkin and when we had further questions later in our stay.
Room is slightly outdated but was very clean and practical. The main room was very large which was a welcome change from other cabins.
Great sites, friendly staff, awesome pools/spas, lots of cabins, clean amenities, reasonable prices
Staff very friendly and helpful. Big park with heaps of space. Just the whole feeling of this place is great.
Best place
Well be staying again. Love the pool, the service and having dogs around to pat and washing machines were great

We liked the park. The reception team members were very efficient and friendly. They provided free bottled water as the general water is from a bore, but is fine. It is well laid out with good bitumen roads, except for one section which was patchy. It has well established shady vegetation throughout. Liked the separate shower rooms with toilet in first amenity block. Gold coin washing machines. Need cash as cards are useless to gains coins at reception. Friendly people staying there. Howard Springs shops 800m away that are very useful,  including Post Office. Roads to Darwin CBD excellent,  but a long drive. Happy experience over several days on powered site. Felt safe, although annoying boom gates are not in use. Recommend as a pleasant park.
What a lovely park. Lots of shade and the pools were amazing.
Great Park. Clean and in fabulous location. Glad we ignored the bad reviews and gossip. Bonus was the Tavern down the road that provided a courtesy bus.

A long way out of town, by choice. Nice and quiet as a result. Sites varied greatly in size, on our third move we were very happy, thanks to an obliging staff. Amenities were good, camp kitchen was adequate,  although a long way from the back of the park.
We stayed in a powerd site,nice and big.
We had a fantastic holiday all services were 5 stars thank you. We hope to be back again
Enjoyed our time at Howard Springs holiday park staff in office 5 stars you ladies are amazing.
A beautiful shady park away from the highway. Shops and tavern nearby. 2 beautiful pools to choose from. It's an easy 30-minute drive to Darwin CBD. Food vans frequent the park also.
Very accommodating, good communication and friendly staff.
Loved our stay here, the park was so peaceful and the amenities cleaned and well kept. The staff were extremely friendly and helpful, would highly recommend.

Disgraceful park, drunk & loud neighbours creating excessive noise well after 10pm every night. Not great when trying to get kids to sleep. Hubby got gastro first night here. Shower runs either burning hot or freezing cold. Not an enjoyable stay and won't be back. The boomgate is not working so no security either. A quiet wikicamps on the side of the road would be a far better option than here.

We had a great stay, reception were lovely once we arrived, a little difficult to get hold of prior to arrival.
We had a nice shady site that was plenty big enough, was a tad awkward as the site boundaries weren’t clear but we just lined up between the trees.
Amenities were cleaned daily but being school holidays, they saw a lot of traffic. Much cleaner when kids aren’t around.
We loved cooking off in the pool and our dog loved the off leash area.
Water provided by office for drinking which we used, we had no illness during our 2 week stay and used all facilities and amenities.

Such a wasted opportunity.  We have been travelling with friends for nearly 3 months and booked 2 adjoining sites.  On arrival 1 site was occupied, a trip to the office resulted in our friends being reallocated a site at the other end of the park.  For the next 3 days we were disturbed by other campers attempting to set up on our site. Several trips to the office and despite assurances from office staff and management that the already occupied adjoining site had been blocked in the system.  During our stay adjacent to the camp kitchen we watched the milk crate 'recycling bin' and the old style garbage bins fill up, on the last day the 'bin birds' managed to spread the contents over the ground and park staff walked over the garbage.  Current management have been there for 14 months and any reasonable person would expect more for the price.
We had an ok experience, while we were there they were having major water issues, which meant we had times with no water. I hope the problem is fixed for the next people staying.

Avoid, avoid, avoid. Terrible issues with contaminated water, impossible to contact via email or phone, Tim the manager is the rudest person working in customer service we've come across.

An unfortunate situation in the gastro spread but the nonexistent response from management has been the real issue. This management should not be running a caravan park. Would not recommend to anyone, even after the water issue is sorted.

We enjoyed our stay at this park.  Loved the spa pools.  Amenties were clean.  Office staff were very friendly & helpful despite having to deal with some rude people concerned about a water issue that was being worked on to be resolved.  We thought it was very generous of management to offer guests bottled water during their stay.

AVOID AT ALL COSTS!!! PRIVACY BREACH AND UNAUTHORISED CREDIT CARD USAGE

Big4 Howard springs charged my credit card 7 days after we had left/checked out, for an extra 9 day stay $280 that we didn't book and were not there for.
we have been trying for 3 weeks to get a refund and been brushed off constantly.we even went back to the park office to get a refund only to be told that they can't issue refunds, head office has to,after contacting big4 head office and being told they will get back to us, they the just direct us back to Howard springs big4.

so we are out of pocket $280 for a stay we didn't stay and didn't book.

They are dealing with a massive gastro outbreak at this park, I would avoid it at all costs, for your and your families own health, confirmed by their own head office as an excuse for the delay.

We stayed in an ensuite site, very run down. The grounds are very run down and messy, needs a lot of work. The park had two great pools and friendly staff, but unfortunately we had extremely bad experience and wouldn’t recommend staying here. Also very noisy permanents. Big 4, this park is screaming for your help!

Great people,  couldn't be more helpful.
Lovely park and not too far away from Darwin

The tourist park was terrible to put it bluntly.
The accommodation was very old & tired, the services were very unreliable.
Several days without running water and power.
Emergency contact after hours phone listed in the cabin connected to a duty manager in regional Victoria!! No clue about any issues raised.
Very old and in need of serious time and money investment to bring up to a reasonable standard.
Web site photos must have been taken several years ago.
Couldn't get out fast enough.
Must be the worst Big4 park in the country.
The new manager is doing a great job. Staff were friendly and the amenities were clean.
Fantastic place everything you need here clean amenities,hot water, friendly people, one of the best parks I’ve been to.

It is a very family friendly place. It allows privacy as well as things for children to do.   With continued care and perseverance the Park should attract many more visitors. The dog park is a great idea.  Having three water attractions entices children to use the facilities.  Howard Springs, just down the road adds to the attractiveness of the Park.  A pleasant venue for a stay.

A really lovely, shady park with plenty of amenities.  The staff here are the most knowledgeable and helpful team I have come across in any caravan park.  They have been able to answer every question we had and have provided us with solutions, recommendations and information.
Friendly staff. Family friendly. Had a few issues with water and power outages, but staff were helpful. Recommend
Great park & lovely staff very happy & accomodating. Amenities good however couple of bathrooms needed a few replacement parts eg soap holder. Highly recommend, pwe stayed 4 nights & felt very safe.

Close to Palmerston but still feels rural.
A cleaner had us waiting an hour after check-in time for a one bedroom with toilet and kitchenette with my 5 year old.
I was upgraded to a 2 bedroom with spa, full kitchen and separate bathroom for the inconvenience.
My child enjoyed the water play area
No hot showers and very little water pressure.Also vans put on sites too small for them making parking up difficult.

Stayed 3 weeks. Had a great time. 2nd time here and will be returning again.
Thanks

Sewage smell unbearable, toilets dirty, bbq not cleaned daily if at all, park untidy, rubbish bins full and rubbish on ground no effort to compact bins and clean up, pools cloudy and by the looks not tested and chemicals not applied at the correct rates (I was told that one pool had to be emptied due to swimmers health) never observed one staff member working at keeping the park clean and tidy

Fantastic seven days with our granddaughters. They loved the peacocks, buggies, pools, waterpark and safe roads to ride their bikes! Our cabin supplied all we needed and was comfortable and clean. We'll  be back😁
The staff were absolutely wonderful and very friendly and helpful. Amenities great, sites clean and large to stay on in an RV.

The worst big 4 caravan park ever, this park is in total disrepair and not fit for human habitation. First day  of 7 day stay on arrival, no ensuite for our site, got told the previous persons had taken the key home, got given another ensuite around the corner, there was no lighting to go around the back to it, also no working light at the stairs to the ensuite, there was no hand rail on the stairs to the ensuite, dirty toilet, shower curtain did not keep water off the floor and made the floor Very slippery.
when exiting ensuite my wife slipped and fell down the stairs in the darkness recieving a terrible bruise, very sore for days.
Reported the incident to staff with no concern from them. We had to ask for our correct ensuite the next day. We received a master key for it as cleaner had spare and could not be found, toilet unclean, blood stain on wall, again floor gets flooded when having a shower, the shower screen is to short, no bath mat, cracked tile's, toilet roll folder falls apart, plastic stool broken and ready for another accident. Reported to staff again, complaints written down but still no action to resolve the issues raised... hopeless.

Go looking for a microwave in the park with the kids for popcorn, go to all the amenities in the park, find out the park has not got (one microwave) in the park walk around the park at night with no lighting in the park tripping over crater hole's all over the park.

Stay well clear, keep driving, also had resent gastro outbreak.

Very disappointed being a big 4 member, money wasted.

Some positives, nice camp kitchen, food vans and live music, closeness to Darwin, pool area was lovely. Some negatives, lack of maintenance in the park, especially the amenities which were not always clean, regardless of time of day. Some showers and toilets were not able to be locked. Shower heads missing. Power outages at the hottest part of the day. All in all a little disappointed in a Big 4 park.
This is the second time staying at Big 4 Howard Springs and we would definitely return. Great location and friendly, efficient staff.

We stayed here for 4 nights and enjoyed our stay. The sites are adequate and the pool was nice. One was a little murky but the smaller pool was great. Kids enjoyed the water park too. They especially loved the free buggies to ride around and the staff were friendly and helpful.
I'm really impressed with this Big4. Lovely big site, amenities are fantastic, staff lovely. Great location. Do recommend. Probably more than we like to spend, but I guess you get that in a big city.
We absolutely love this park, so close to town and yet so convenient. The one issue we’re the three little birds that kept everyone awake every night. Move them on and it would be faultless.
Terrible
Never dissatisfied, always great service

I very rarely complain about parks but as I have stayed here before and was happy we went again. Dogs not being kept quiet was the main complaint,little happy things that bark at every other dog never being chastised and being left out overnight. The answer to my complaint was do you want to move sites. Plenty of sites that the problem people could have been sent to not the people who are doing the right thing. Another 2 couples around us were extremely unhappy also. Another problem was the disabled toilet that was being used by others including me as a quick in out was noticeably getting very unclean with sanitary items etc in bin. This hadn’t been cleaned for a few days. When I noticed that the cleaner was in the main stream bathroom I got my husband to go and ask about it. Apparently he was unaware that there was a disabled bathroom,how does that happen
Beautiful park, good facilities and super friendly staff
Very nice staff. Amenities not great. Cold shower, almost zero water pressure. Main bbq no gas. Not really worth $85 for a powered site. Especially for a big 4
Great Big Park, plenty of room for Vans, and allot of shade 😎 to relax, and a Beatiful big swimming pool / 2 x spa's, Splash Park, kids playground. Very relaxing Park

We've just spent 3 weeks here and absolutely loved our stay.  Great location outside of town, felt very safe, but also easy access to shops and attractions.  The sites are lovely and big, the amenities are nice and clean and the pool is really lovely, we spent everyday in there.  The staff are wonderful, very accommodating with requests and do an amazing job around the park.
Great facilities, very friendly staff, lovely setting . Close to the Tavern that was fabulous,  great relaxing time , will be visiting next time thank you
